节点.功能描述: Free scrolling is supported.

节点.功能描述:
Defines a controller for scrollable container components.

NOTE
1. The binding of a Scroller instance to a scrollable container component occurs during the component creation phase.
2. Scroller APIs can only be effectively called after the Scroller instance is bound to a scrollable container component. Otherwise, depending on the API called, it may have no effect or throw an exception.
3. For example, with aboutToAppear, this callback is executed after a new instance of a custom component is created and before its build() method is called. Therefore, if a scrollable component is defined within the build method of a custom component, the internal scrollable component has not yet been created during the aboutToAppear callback of that custom component, and therefore the Scroller APIs cannot be called effectively.
A constructor used to create a Scroller object.
Scrolls to the specified position. Anonymous Object Rectification.
注释信息: @param { ScrollOptions } options - Parameters for scrolling to the specified position.

parameters.说明: Parameters for scrolling to the specified position.

节点.功能描述: Called when the setting slides to the specified position.

节点.功能描述:
Performs inertial scrolling based on the initial velocity passed in.
NOTE
If the value specified is 0, it is considered as invalid, and the scrolling for this instance will not take effect. A positive value indicates scrolling towards the top, while a negative value indicates scrolling towards the bottom.

节点.功能描述: Scrolls to the next or previous page.
Called when page turning mode is set.
Scrolls to the next or previous page. The value true means to scroll to the next page, and false means to scroll to the previous page.

@param { Axis } direction - Scrolling direction: horizontal or vertical.

parameters.说明: Whether to turn to the next page.

节点.功能描述:
Scrolls to a specified index, with support for setting an extra offset for the scroll.

NOTE
This API only works for the ArcList, Grid, List, and WaterFlow components.


NOTE
If the value set is a negative value or greater than the maximum index of the items in the container, the value is deemed abnormal, and no scrolling will be performed. The value true means to enable that the smooth animation, and false means the opposite.
Default value: false
Default value when the container is List: ScrollAlign.START
Default value when the container is Grid: ScrollAlign.AUTO
Default value when the container is WaterFlow: ScrollAlign.START
NOTE
This parameter is only available for the List, Grid, and WaterFlow components. for example, an extra offset for the scroll.
Default value: 0, in vp
注释信息: @param { number } value - Index of the item to be scrolled to in the container.
@param { boolean } [smooth] - Whether to enable the smooth animation for scrolling to the item with the specified index.
@param { ScrollAlign } [align] - How the list item to scroll to is aligned with the container.
@param { ScrollToIndexOptions } [options] - Options for scrolling to a specified index,

parameters.说明: Index of the item to be scrolled to in the container.
ScrollToIndexOptions } [options] - Options for scrolling to a specified index,

@param { ScrollToIndexOptions } [options] - Sets the options of a specified index, such as extra offset.

parameters.说明: ScrollToIndexOptions } [options] - Sets the options of a specified index, such as extra offset.

节点.功能描述: Provides parameters for scrolling to a specific position in a scrollable container.

Horizontal scrolling offset. Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
This parameter cannot be set in percentage.
If the value is less than 0, the offset will be 0 for non-animated scrolling. Animated scrolling stops at the starting position by default. By setting the animation parameter, you can enable a bounce effect when the scrolling goes beyond the boundary.
This parameter takes effect only when the scroll axis is the x-axis.

Vertical scrolling offset. Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
This parameter cannot be set in percentage.
If the value is less than 0, the offset will be 0 for non-animated scrolling. Animated scrolling stops at the starting position by default. By setting the animation parameter, you can enable a bounce effect when the scrolling goes beyond the boundary.
This parameter takes effect only when the scroll axis is the y-axis.

Animation configuration Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
Currently, the List, Scroll, Grid, and WaterFlow support the Boolean type and ICurve.

and the boolean type enables default spring animation.
注释信息: @type { ?( ScrollAnimationOptions | boolean) } The ScrollAnimationOptions type provides custom animation parameters
@default ScrollAnimationOptions: { duration: 1000, curve: Curve.Ease, canOverScroll: false }

节点.功能描述: Set whether the scroll target position can over the boundary.
注释信息: @type { ?boolean } whether the scroll target position can over the boundary.

节点.功能描述: Provides parameters for page scrolling behavior.
Whether to turn to the next page.The value true means to scroll to the next page, and false means to scroll to the previous page.
Whether to enable the page-turning animation.The value true means to enable the page-turning animation, and false means the opposite.
Defines a scroll snapping mode object.
Alignment mode for the scroll snap position.

Pagination points for scroll snapping.

NOTE
1. If the value is of the Dimension type, it indicates the size of each page, and the system will paginate based on this size.
2. If the value is of the Array\ type, each Dimension represents a pagination point, and the system will paginate accordingly. Each Dimension value must be within the [0, scrollable distance] range.
3. If this parameter is not set or Dimension is set to a value less than or equal to 0, the value is regarded as an invalid value. In this case, there is no scroll snapping. When the value is of the Array\ type, the items in the array must be monotonically increasing.
4. When the value is a percentage, the actual size is the product of the viewport of the Scroll component and the percentage value.

Whether to enable the snap to start feature. When scroll snapping is defined for the Scroll component, setting this parameter to false enables the component to scroll between the start and the first page.

NOTE
2. This attribute takes effect only when snapPagination is set to a value of the Array\ type; it does not work with values of the Dimension type.

Whether to enable the snap to end feature. When scroll snapping is defined for the Scroll component, setting this parameter to false enables the component to scroll between the end and the last page.

NOTE
2. This attribute takes effect only when snapPagination is set to a value of the Array\ type; it does not work with values of the Dimension type.

Called when a scrollable container is set.
The data returned by the event handler when onScrollFrameBegin.

节点.功能描述:
Triggered to return the horizontal and vertical offsets, in vp, during scrolling when the specified scroll event occurs.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is started by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called.
3. This event supports the out-of-bounds bounce effect.

Triggered before scrolling.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is started by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called.
3. This event supports the out-of-bounds bounce effect.
注释信息: @param { ScrollOnWillScrollCallback } handler - Callback triggered before scrolling.

节点.功能描述:
Triggered when the Scroll component scrolls.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is started by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called.
3. This event supports the out-of-bounds bounce effect.
注释信息: @param { ScrollOnScrollCallback } handler - Callback triggered when the Scroll component scrolls.

parameters.说明: Callback triggered when the Scroll component scrolls.

节点.功能描述:
Triggered when scrolling reaches the edge. Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling reaches the edge after being started by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called.
3. This event supports the out-of-bounds bounce effect.
注释信息: @param { OnScrollEdgeCallback } event - Edge position to scroll to.

节点.功能描述:
Called when scrolling start. Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is started by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called, accompanied by a transition animation.
注释信息: @param { VoidCallback } event - Callback triggered when scrolling starts.

节点.功能描述:
Triggered when scrolling stops.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is stopped by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called, accompanied by a transition animation.

Called when scrolling has stopped. Anonymous Object Rectification.

NOTE
1. This event is triggered when scrolling is stopped by the Scroll component or other input settings, such as keyboard and mouse operations.
2. This event is triggered when the controller API is called, accompanied by a transition animation.
注释信息: @param { VoidCallback } event - Callback triggered when scrolling stops.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the effect used when the scroll boundary is reached.

@param { EdgeEffectOptions } options - Whether to enable the scroll effect when the component content is smaller than the component itself.

节点.功能描述:
Sets the nested scrolling options. You can set the nested scrolling mode in the forward and backward directions to implement scrolling linkage with the parent component.
Default value: { scrollForward: NestedScrollMode.SELF_ONLY, scrollBackward: NestedScrollMode.SELF_ONLY }
注释信息: @returns { ScrollAttribute } the attribute of the scroll.

节点.功能描述: Sets whether to support scroll gestures. When this attribute is set to false, scrolling by finger or mouse is not supported, but the scroll controller API is not affected.

Default value: true
@returns { ScrollAttribute } The attribute of the scroll

parameters.说明:
Whether to support scroll gestures.
Default value: true

节点.功能描述:
Sets the friction coefficient. It applies only to gestures in the scrolling area, and it affects only indirectly the scroll chaining during the inertial scrolling process. If this attribute is set to a value less than or equal to 0, the default value is used.
Default value: 0.9 for wearable devices and 0.6 for non-wearable devices
Since API version 11, the default value for non-wearable devices is 0.7.
Since API version 12, the default value for non-wearable devices is 0.75.
注释信息: @returns { ScrollAttribute } the attribute of the scroll.

节点.功能描述: Sets the scroll snapping mode.
注释信息: @returns { ScrollAttribute } the attribute of the scroll.

@returns { ScrollAttribute } the attribute of the scroll.

parameters.说明: Whether to enable the swipe-to-turn-pages feature. Default value: false.

节点.功能描述:
Represents the callback triggered when the Scroll component scrolls.

NOTE
If the onScrollFrameBegin event and scrollBy method are used to implement nested scrolling, set the edgeEffect attribute of the scrollable child component to None. For example, if a List is nested in the Scroll component, edgeEffect of the List must be set to EdgeEffect.None.

and a negative offset indicates scrolling to the right.
Unit: vp A positive offset indicates scrolling upward, and a negative offset indicates scrolling downward.
Unit: vp
注释信息: @param { number } xOffset - Horizontal offset per frame during scrolling. A positive offset indicates scrolling to the left,
@param { number } yOffset - Vertical offset per frame during scrolling.
